title: Researcher
description: Developed methods for computer-aided proof of a conjecture on sharp bounds on the JLV algorithm for tensor decomposition.
Devised method for orbit recovery in multi-reference alignment variants using variety constrained linear systems.
Applied these algorithms to achieve orbit recovery in dihedral multi-reference alignment.

title: Researcher
description: Exploring inverse problems related to imaging and geometric tomography for UCD4IDS.

Determining the effect of dose-fractionation on spectral method for phase retrieval in X-ray diffraction using the non-asymptotic random matrix theory for spiked Wishart distributions (manuscript in progress).

title: Instructor
description: Planned lectures and assignments to teach key linear algebra concepts.
Designed small weekly assessments to evaluate students in a lower-stakes manner.

title: Research Software Developer
description: Implemented a robust version of the simultaneous diagonalization algorithm for tensor decomposition.
Implemented a heretofore theoretical algorithm for computing the intersection of a linear subspace with a conic variety.
Applied these algorithms to achieve orbit recovery in dihedral multi-reference alignment.
Use unit testing to validate results.

title: Frontend Developer
description: Created new graphical user interfaces for C++ driven web applications in the header-only library, Empirical.
Suggested a new schema for component inheritance to ensure that prefabricated web components are extensible.
Presented at 2021 BEACON conference, with proof-of-concept using C++ code live.

title: Instructional Materials Editor
description: Reviewed and tested computer lab projects designed to teach students basic data science and calculus.
Verified and refined R programs to import and process data, plot functions and run simulations.
Analyzed the alignment of course objectives and course materials.

title: Frontend Developer
description: Planned interface for a progressive web-app to facilitate sharing physical books between neighbors.
Designed UI and control flow for loans management by users.
Practice coding sprints using Jira to break down and assign development tasks to coworkers.

title: Frontend Development Intern
description: Created front-end components in React for a communications web application faithful to user interface and experience design specifications.
Wrote unit tests for backend functions in C# to store and retrieve user data from a SQL database.

title: Technical Writer/Researcher
description: Worked in a team of 3 to write and submit a solution paper in 5 days (Problem D, Team #1908810).
Leveraged spectral graph theory and agent-based modeling to model flow on a network.
Identified potential bottlenecks in the Louvre's floor-plan and gave recommendations for an evacuation strategy.
Submission ranked in top 1% of 11,262 entries.

title: Programmer
description: Wrote an open source application in Javascript with the Processing library to simulate flocking behavior.
Generate user-interface for interaction matrices to explore complex emergent behavior and group dynamics.
